title: Magnesium Daily Requirement Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate your daily magnesium intake (RDA: 310–420mg). Improves sleep, reduces muscle cramps & migraines. Food sources and supplement guide.
seoKeywords: how much magnesium do I need
intro: Magnesium participates in over 300 enzyme reactions: ATP production, protein synthesis, muscle contraction, heart rhythm, and neurological function. The IOM RDA is 320 mg/day for women and 420 mg/day for men (ages 19–50). An estimated 50% of the U.S. population consumes less than the RDA—a similar figure in Argentina. Deficiency is linked to muscle cramps, insomnia, migraines, insulin resistance, hypertension, and osteoporosis. This calculator gives your personalized daily dose based on age and sex, plus supplementation forms: citrate for constipation, glycinate for sleep, malate for fatigue. Prioritize food sources: almonds (270mg/100g), spinach (79mg), cocoa (500mg), beans (130mg).
keyTakeaway: Magnesium: 320mg women / 420mg men (ages 19–50). Almonds 270mg, cocoa 500mg. 50% of population is deficient.

example.title: Example: 35-Year-Old Male
steps: **Reach it**: 30g almonds (80mg) + 1 cup spinach (80mg) + 1 tsp cocoa (80mg) + 1 cup beans (130mg) = 370 mg + 50mg supplement.
example.result: You need **420 mg/day**. With nuts + vegetables + legumes you'll hit ~90%, the rest from dark chocolate or a supplement.

explanation:
## What Is Magnesium?

A mineral cofactor in over 300 enzymatic reactions.

## IOM RDA Guidelines

| Age Group | mg/day |
|---|---|
| Men 19–30 | 400 |
| Men 31+ | 420 |
| Women 19–30 | 310 |
| Women 31+ | 320 |
| Pregnancy | 350–400 |
| Lactation | 310–360 |

## Top Magnesium Foods

| Food | Magnesium (mg/100g) |
|---|---|
| Pumpkin seeds | 535 |
| Dark chocolate (70%) | 228 |
| Almonds | 270 |
| Cocoa powder | 500 |
| Cooked spinach | 79 |
| Cashews | 292 |
| Black beans | 171 |
| Avocado | 29 |
| Banana | 27 |

## Magnesium Supplement Forms

| Form | Best For | Absorption |
|---|---|---|
| Citrate | Constipation relief | High |
| Glycinate | Sleep & anxiety | High, no laxative effect |
| Malate | Energy & fatigue | High |
| Oxide | General laxative | **Low** (40%) |
| L-threonate | Cognitive support | High, crosses BBB |

## Health Benefits

- Fewer muscle cramps.
- Better deep sleep.
- 22% reduction in migraine frequency.
- Improved HbA1c.
- Lower blood pressure.

## Common Mistakes

1. Cheap magnesium oxide (poor absorption).
2. Taking high doses at once (causes diarrhea).
3. Relying only on chocolate.
4. Taking with antibiotics (interferes with absorption).

## Related Calculators

- [Daily Potassium Calculator](/calculadora-potasio-diario-necesario).
- [Daily Calcium Calculator](/calculadora-calcio-diario-edad).
- [Pre/Post Workout Electrolytes](/calculadora-electrolitos-pre-post-entreno).

Consult a doctor if you take diuretics or proton pump inhibitors (PPIs).

faq.q: How much magnesium do I need daily?
faq.a: **320 mg for women / 420 mg for men** (ages 19–50). Requirements vary by life stage.

faq.q: What's the best form of magnesium supplement?
faq.a: **Glycinate** for sleep; **citrate** for constipation. Avoid oxide (only 40% absorption).

faq.q: Should I take a magnesium supplement?
faq.a: Yes, if you eat few vegetables or nuts. 200–400 mg daily is safe for most people.

faq.q: Can magnesium help with muscle cramps?
faq.a: Yes—deficiency is a common cause. **300 mg/day** often resolves cramping.
Yes. Glycinate and L-threonate are best for sleep quality and duration.

faq.q: What are the side effects of magnesium?
faq.a: High doses cause diarrhea. **Max 350 mg/day as a supplement** (tolerable upper limit).

faq.q: What foods are highest in magnesium?
faq.a: Pumpkin seeds (535mg/100g), cocoa powder (500mg), almonds (270mg), black beans (171mg).
Yes: PPIs, thiazide diuretics, and antibiotics (quinolones, tetracyclines) can interfere with absorption.

sources.name: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
